content.text: The fixture idiom is clear. But before I write the collision case, I need to resolve a real subtlety: ensconce mints a *fresh* touchmark from the current time each call, so two CLI ensconces normally produce two *different* Lodes — no collision. To test the collision path I need to know whether the touchmark can be pinned. Let me read the host-side ensconce:

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
24	# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
30	# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
31	# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
35	
36	# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
37	# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
38	# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
39	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
40	# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
41	# Args: token origin stamp
42	zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
43	  zrbld_sentinel
44	
45	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
46	  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
47	  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"
48	
49	  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
50	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
51	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
52	
53	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
54	  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
55	  local -r z_recipe=(
56	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
57	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
58	  )
59	
60	  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
61	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
62	  jq -n \
63	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
64	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
65	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
66	    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
67	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
68	    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
69	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
70	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
71	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
72	    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
73	    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
74	    '{
75	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
76	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
77	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
78	      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
79	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
80	      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
81	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
82	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
83	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
84	      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
85	      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
86	      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
87	      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
88	      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
89	      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
90	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
91	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"
92	
93	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
94	    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
95	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
96	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
97	}
98	
99	# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
100	# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
101	# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
102	# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
103	# bole's shape, not the spine's.
104	zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
105	  zrbld_sentinel
106	
107	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
108	
109	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
110	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
111	
112	  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
113	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
114	
115	  local z_n=""
116	  local z_slot_key=""
117	  local z_stamp_file=""
118	  local z_stamp=""
119	  local z_vouch_file=""
120	  local z_vouch=""
121	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
122	    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
123	    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
124	    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
125	      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
126	    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
127	    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue
128	
129	    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
130	    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
131	      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
132	    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
133	    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
134	
135	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
136	    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
137	  done
138	}
139	
140	######################################################################
141	# External Functions (rbld_*)
142	
143	rbld_ensconce() {
144	  zrbld_sentinel
145	
146	  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
147	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
148	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
149	
150	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
151	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
152	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
153	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
154	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
155	
156	  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
157	  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
158	  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
159	  local z_origin=""
160	  local z_origin_count=0
161	  local z_n=""
162	  local z_origin_var=""
163	  local z_slot_origin=""
164	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
165	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
166	    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
167	    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
168	    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
169	    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
170	  done
171	
172	  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
173	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
174	  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
175	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"
176	
177	  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
178	  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
179	  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
180	    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"
181	
182	  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"
183	
184	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
185	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
186	
187	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
188	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
189	
190	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
191	  local z_token=""
192	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
193	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
194	
195	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
196	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
197	  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
198	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
199	
200	  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
201	  zrbld_ensconce_extract
202	
203	  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
204	}
205	
206	# eof

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.

set -euo pipefail

# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
# Args: token origin stamp
zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
# bole's shape, not the spine's.
zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local z_n=""
  local z_slot_key=""
  local z_stamp_file=""
  local z_stamp=""
  local z_vouch_file=""
  local z_vouch=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue

    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_ensconce() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
  local z_origin=""
  local z_origin_count=0
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_slot_origin=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
  done

  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"

  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"

  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_ensconce_extract

  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

content.text: Now I see the crux. The stamp is minted at `rbldb_Bole.sh:197` from `BURD_NOW_STAMP` (a regime timestamp), 1-second granularity. So forcing a *collision* (two different images on one touchmark) deterministically hinges on whether `BURD_NOW_STAMP` can be pinned. Let me check:

input.command: echo "=== where is BURD_NOW_STAMP set / is it env-overridable? ==="; grep -rn "BURD_NOW_STAMP" /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "rbldb_Bole" | head -30
input.description: Trace BURD_NOW_STAMP definition and overridability

content.text:
This is the real subtlety the docket flagged — and it's sharper than expected. `bud_dispatch.sh:98` regenerates `BURD_NOW_STAMP` every invocation, and ensconce derives the touchmark as `bYYMMDDHHMMSS` (1-second granularity) from it. Two lifecycle ensconces run **minutes apart** (cloud builds are slow), so they always get **different** stamps → different Lodes. **The guard's collision and idempotent branches are unreachable from back-to-back CLI ensconces** — only the "fresh" branch fires. The docket's "second ensconce *reusing a touchmark*" quietly presumes a touchmark-pin the system doesn't currently expose.

Let me confirm my bash guard at least lints clean before I surface the test fork:

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURE Regime - Bash Utility Regime Environment Module
#
# BURE is an ambient regime — variables are set in the environment by callers,
# not sourced from a file. Callers export BURE_* variables before invoking.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURE_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zbure_*)

zbure_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
  # Exception: ambient regime defaults for optional behavioral overrides.
  BURE_VERBOSE="${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}"
  BURE_COLOR="${BURE_COLOR:-auto}"
  BURE_COUNTDOWN="${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_NAME="${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE:-}"
  BURE_LABEL="${BURE_LABEL:-}"

  # Enroll all BURE variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURE

  buv_group_enroll "Behavioral Overrides"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_COUNTDOWN   0  4  "Countdown override (skip to disable)"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_VERBOSE     "Verbosity level" 0 1 2 3
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_COLOR       "Color mode" auto 0 1

  buv_group_enroll "Tweak Mechanism"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_NAME  0  64   "Tweak name (consumer-interpreted)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE 0  256  "Tweak value (consumer-interpreted)"

  buv_group_enroll "Exchange Labels"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_LABEL       0  120  "Correlation label for cross-project exchange (xname format)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURE_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURE BURE_

  # Lock all enrolled BURE_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURE

  readonly ZBURE_KINDLED=1
}

zbure_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bure not kindled - call zbure_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURE enrollment validations
zbure_enforce() {
  zbure_sentinel

  buv_vet BURE

  # Custom enforce: BURE_COUNTDOWN must be empty or "skip"
  if test -n "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}"; then
    test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}" = "skip" \
      || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or empty, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
  fi
}

# eof

content.text:
Investigated fully — and your instinct is right. Here's the plain finding.

## No, there's no sprue constraint on tweak names today

`bure_regime.sh:56` enrolls it as a **free string**:
```
buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_NAME  0  64  "Tweak name (consumer-interpreted)"
```
BUS0 confirms it's deliberate: the tweak flows *"without BUK interpreting its semantics"* — BUK length-checks (≤64) and scope-checks, nothing more. The only "validation" is each consumer's literal compare (`!= "threemodegraft"`).

And the drift is already visible: the real consumer (`rbfd`) checks **`threemodegraft`**, but the BURE self-test exercises the mechanism with **`graft_image`**. Two names, no registry tying them together.

## Why this blocks the test (the hazard you're guarding against)

If my fixture exports `BURE_TWEAK_NAME="ensconcestamp"` and the `rbld_ensconce` consumer checks for `"ensconce_stamp"` (one typo), the tweak **silently no-ops** → ensconce mints a fresh time-stamp → no collision → and my test would **pass for the wrong reason**. A free-string name has no backstop against that. That's exactly why a sprue belongs here *before* I add a second consumer.

## The fix — and it's the BUK-native sprue

BUK already has the mechanism: `bus_enum_sprue` (BUS0:997) — an **enum-enrolled** value is "a sprue in the MCM sense." BURE already enum-enrolls `BURE_VERBOSE`/`BURE_COLOR`. So the repair is to convert the tweak name from `buv_string_enroll` to `buv_enum_enroll` over a governed set:
```
buv_enum_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_NAME  "Tweak name (sprued)"  threemodegraft  ensconcestamp
```
Now an unregistered/typo'd name **fails enforcement loudly** (`buc_die`) instead of silently no-op'ing.

## Two decisions this raises — your call

1. **Layering.** An enum in BURE means BUK's regime enumerates *consumer* tweak names (`threemodegraft` is RBK's, `ensconcestamp` is RBK's). That centralizes the registry (good — one place, cross-kit collision-proof) but couples BUK to consumer names (a smell). The alternative is a weaker prefix-shape sprue that keeps BUK generic but won't catch a within-shape typo. Your word "sprue" points at the enum; I want to confirm you accept the coupling.
2. **Ripple.** The self-test's `graft_image` placeholder would need to either join the enum or be swapped for a registered name.

## Scope

This is a **BUK-regime change**, outside ₢BHAAC's collision-guard scope — but a genuine prerequisite for using bure_tweak safely. I'd treat it as a small precursor (its own notch, possibly worth a spook note since it's a latent gap that predates this pace).

**Where things stand on ₢BHAAC:** code guard done + lints clean; RBSLE spec done; the test is parked on this decision.

How do you want to play it — enum-sprue in BURE (accepting the coupling), and fold it in as a precursor notch, or handle it some other way?

message.content: The intent of driving all tweak values to have sprues enables this to function without a central location: we 'virtually' build the registry by grepping for a tweak specific selection prefix.  Thoughts?  MEans probably ought to be 'buXXX_' namespaced though critically never placed in bu.  Could be exotic case like buTrb_ tying Tweak to rb project?

content.text:
This is a better design than my enum, and it dissolves the exact objection I raised. Let me think it through with you.

**The core move is right — and it's more in-grain than my central enum.** "Virtual registry by grep" is how the whole codebase already works: you enumerate a namespace by grepping its prefix, not by reading a master list. A central BURE enum would have rotted and coupled BUK to consumer names; a greppable sprue prefix has neither problem. You improved the idea — I was reaching for a registry when the project's native answer is a *reserved prefix*.

**The unlock that kills the coupling:** BUK enforces the **shape**, never the **names**. `zbure_enforce` requires a non-empty `BURE_TWEAK_NAME` to match `^buT[a-z]+_` (the sprue) — and that's it. BUK validates that a tweak name *is sprued*; it never learns that `…ensconce_stamp` is RBK's. So BUK stays fully generic, an unsprued/garbage name fails loud, and the grep-registry is guaranteed complete (nothing escapes the prefix). That's the thing my enum couldn't give without contaminating BUK. The "BUK doesn't interpret semantics" line in BUS0 survives intact — a prefix is shape, not semantics.

**On `buTrb_` specifically — I mostly endorse it, with one reservation to name.** The capital `T` is doing real work: lowercase `but_` is already the test module (`but`/`butt`/`buts` are a dense family), so `buT` *deliberately* jumps out of that crowded lowercase space while staying mnemonic for Tweak. That's clever, not arbitrary. The scheme reads cleanly: `bu`(BUK owns the mechanism) + `T`(Tweak) + `rb`(consumer project) + `_name`, so `grep buT` finds every tweak and `grep buTrb_` finds RB's.

My one reservation: it introduces a **case-distinguished prefix**, which the module namespace doesn't otherwise use — `buT` collides with `but` under any case-*insensitive* grep, and is mistypeable. It's safe only if the enforcement regex and our greps stay case-sensitive, and the convention is written down. I think the mnemonic + collision-dodge earns the case-novelty for a narrow seam channel — but it *is* a new axis in the naming system, so it should clear your own load-bearing bar consciously, not by default.

**One gap the sprue alone doesn't close:** two correctly-*sprued* names that differ in the suffix (`buTrb_ensconce_stamp` vs `…_stmp`) still silently mismatch between test and consumer. The sprue governs the namespace; it doesn't catch a typo *within* it. So I'd pair it with the existing mirror discipline — the bash consumer defines the name as a constant, the Rust test references the mirror — so there's one spelling, not two. Sprue + shared-constant together is the full protection.

**Two wrinkles before this is a clean scheme:**
1. **BUK's own tweaks.** The self-test's `graft_image`/`threemodegraft` are BUK exercising the mechanism — but the project segment assumes a *consumer* project, and `buTbu_` is degenerate (bu twice, and bare-bu is forbidden). The scheme needs a story for BUK-owned/self-test tweaks (a reserved segment like `buTst_`?).
2. **Retrofit + record.** `threemodegraft` (rbfd) should convert to the sprue too, or the registry has a hole on day one. And the convention belongs in the CLAUDE.md mint discipline (the Extended Namespace Checklist already lists env vars, refs, slash commands — tweak names join it) plus the BUS0 tweak section.

**Where I land:** convinced on grep-sprue + BUK shape-enforcement + shared-constant; that trio is genuinely better than my enum. The live calls I'd want you to settle: (a) accept the capital-`T` case-novelty, or pick a lowercase prefix and lose the T-mnemonic; (b) the BUK-self-test segment; (c) scope — this is a BUK-mechanism + convention + retrofit change, a real precursor pace to ₢BHAAC, not something to smuggle into the collision-guard notch.

What's your read on the capital-T tradeoff — worth it, or does the case-collision-with-`but` bother you enough to go lowercase?

echo

echo

  printf 'bu%s : %s\n' "$L" "${c:-<free>}"
done

content.text:
Fished the whole `bu<X>` space — and the data corrected my own assumption (I'd have guessed `q`/`x` were free; they're both **taken** — `buq_`/`BUQ_` and `bux_`/`zbux_` exist). Here's the real map.

**Genuinely free single-letter slots:** `bua`, `bue`, `bug`, `bum`, `buo` (zero identifier hits across `Tools/` + `tt/`). Everything else (`buc/bud/buf/buh/bui*/buj/buk/bul/bun/bup/buq/bur/bus/but/buu/buv/buw/bux/buy/buz/bub`) is occupied or grep-polluted.

**But "free" isn't enough — the slot has to grep *clean***, since the whole point is a virtual registry via `grep`. That knocks out most of them:

| Slot | Verdict |
|------|---------|
| `bug` | ❌ free but `grep bug` drowns in `debug`/`bugfix`/`debugging` — registry unusable |
| `bui` | ❌ swamped by `build`/`builder` |
| `bun` | ❌ swamped by `bundle` |
| `bum` | ~ free, low pollution, but the word "bum" is a small wince in `bumrb_` |
| `bue` | ✅ free, clean (`blue`/`value` don't contain `bue`), neutral |
| `buo` | ✅ free, clean (`buoy` is rare in code), neutral, and — checked — does **not** collide with `buto_` (the BUT test-output family) under case-sensitive grep |
| `bua` | ✅ likely free (absent from the histogram), clean |

**My pick: `buo`.** It's free, the grep stays clean, it's case-stable (no `but`/`buT` hazard), it carries no unfortunate word, and it doesn't collide with the dense `but*` test family. Swapping it into your project-namespaced shape:

```
buo<proj>_<name>     →   buorb_ensconce_stamp
grep buo             →   every tweak, everywhere
grep buorb_          →   RB's tweaks
```

`bue` is the clean runner-up if `buo` reads oddly to you; `bua` if you want a third.

Everything else we settled carries over unchanged: BUK enforces the **shape** (`^buo[a-z]+_`) not the names (stays generic, no coupling); pair with a shared consumer/test constant for the suffix; and the BUK-self-test still needs a segment story (`buobu_` is degenerate — probably a reserved `buost_` for BUK's own).

Which letter do you want — `buo`, or one of the alternates?

input.content:
# jjezs_slate buo-tweak-sprue

## Character
Infrastructure precursor to the ensconce collision-guard (₢BHAAC's live-GAR test needs a stamp-pin tweak). Touches a shared BUK regime — mechanical but exacting. Discovered mid-mount: BURE tweak names are ungoverned free strings (only `threemodegraft` exists today), so a test/consumer name typo silently no-ops and the test passes for the wrong reason.

## Goal
Give BURE tweak names a sprue so an unregistered/typo'd name fails loud instead of silently no-op'ing, and bring every existing tweak under it.

## Locked (settled in conversation)
- **Sprue letter `buo`** (free, neutral). Shape `buo<proj>_<name>` (e.g. `buorb_…`). The `_`-bearing sprue IS a virtual registry: `grep buo` finds every tweak, `grep buo<proj>_` one project's — no central enum/list to rot.
- **BUK enforces SHAPE, never names.** `zbure_enforce`: a non-empty `BURE_TWEAK_NAME` must match `^buo[a-z]+_`, else die. Keeps BURE generic (no consumer-name coupling) and preserves the BUS0 "BUK doesn't interpret tweak semantics" line — shape ≠ semantics.
- **Suffix-typo guard is the shared constant**, not the sprue: each consumer defines its tweak name as a constant; the test references the mirror (existing mirror discipline). Sprue governs the namespace; the constant kills spelling drift.
- **BUK's own (self-test) tweaks** use a reserved segment, not a consumer project (`buobu_` is degenerate) — settle the segment at mount.

## Transform all existing tweaks
None left bare. Discovery: `grep -rn 'BURE_TWEAK_NAME' Tools/` — covers the `threemodegraft` consumer (rbfd), its setter in the theurge harness, and the BURE self-test's example values.

## Register the convention
CLAUDE.md mint discipline (Extended Namespace Checklist — tweak names join env-vars / refs / slash-commands) + the BUS0 tweak section.

## Done
BURE rejects a non-sprued tweak name loud; every existing tweak is `buo`-sprued; BUK self-test + the rbfd consumer path stay green; the convention is documented; a sprued stamp-pin tweak is available for ₢BHAAC.

file.content:
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURE Regime - Bash Utility Regime Environment Module
#
# BURE is an ambient regime — variables are set in the environment by callers,
# not sourced from a file. Callers export BURE_* variables before invoking.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURE_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zbure_*)

zbure_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
  # Exception: ambient regime defaults for optional behavioral overrides.
  BURE_VERBOSE="${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}"
  BURE_COLOR="${BURE_COLOR:-auto}"
  BURE_COUNTDOWN="${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_NAME="${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE:-}"
  BURE_LABEL="${BURE_LABEL:-}"

  # Enroll all BURE variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURE

  buv_group_enroll "Behavioral Overrides"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_COUNTDOWN   0  4  "Countdown override (skip to disable)"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_VERBOSE     "Verbosity level" 0 1 2 3
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_COLOR       "Color mode" auto 0 1

  buv_group_enroll "Tweak Mechanism"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_NAME  0  64   "Tweak name (consumer-interpreted)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE 0  256  "Tweak value (consumer-interpreted)"

  buv_group_enroll "Exchange Labels"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_LABEL       0  120  "Correlation label for cross-project exchange (xname format)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURE_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURE BURE_

  # Lock all enrolled BURE_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURE

  readonly ZBURE_KINDLED=1
}

zbure_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bure not kindled - call zbure_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURE enrollment validations
zbure_enforce() {
  zbure_sentinel

  buv_vet BURE

  # Custom enforce: BURE_COUNTDOWN must be empty or "skip"
  if test -n "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}"; then
    test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}" = "skip" \
      || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or empty, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
  fi
}

# eof

input.old_string:
# Custom enforce: BURE_COUNTDOWN must be empty or "skip"
  if test -n "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}"; then
    test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}" = "skip" \
      || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or empty, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"

input.new_string:
# Custom enforce: BURE_COUNTDOWN must be empty or "skip"
  if test -n "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}"; then
    test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}" = "skip" \
      || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or empty, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
  fi

  # Custom enforce: BURE_TWEAK_NAME, when set, must carry the buo tweak sprue —
  # a `buo<segment>_` prefix. BUK validates the SHAPE only (the virtual registry
  # of tweaks is `grep buo`); it never enumerates consumer names, so this stays
  # generic. An unregistered or mistyped tweak name fails loud here rather than
  # silently no-op'ing at the consumer.
  if test -n "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME}"; then
    case "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME}" in
      buo[a-z]*_*) : ;;
      *) buc_die "BURE_TWEAK_NAME must carry the buo sprue (buo<segment>_<name>), got '${BURE_TWEAK_NAME}'" ;;
    esac
  fi
}

The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.

The sprue doubles as a virtual registry: `grep buo` enumerates every tweak across the codebase, with no central list to maintain.
+
Example: `buorb_graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

file.content:
### Extended Namespace Checklist

When minting, enumerate ALL namespaces the system touches:

| Namespace | Pattern | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Git refs | `refs/{prefix}/...` | `refs/vvg/locks/*` |
| Slash commands | `/{prefix}-{noun}` | `/vvc-commit` |
| Command files | `.claude/commands/{cmd}.md` | `vvc-commit.md` |
| Environment vars | `{PREFIX}_NAME` | `VVG_SIZE_LIMIT` |
| Target repo paths | `Tools/{kit}/...` | `Tools/vvk/bin/vvx` |

This is not exhaustive. The principle: **any persistent name anywhere is in the mint universe.**

### Kit Infrastructure Suffixes

input.old_string:
| Environment vars | `{PREFIX}_NAME` | `VVG_SIZE_LIMIT` |
| Target repo paths | `Tools/{kit}/...` | `Tools/vvk/bin/vvx` |

This is not exhaustive. The principle: **any persistent name anywhere is in the mint universe.**
input.new_string:
| Environment vars | `{PREFIX}_NAME` | `VVG_SIZE_LIMIT` |
| Target repo paths | `Tools/{kit}/...` | `Tools/vvk/bin/vvx` |
| BURE tweak names | `buo{proj}_{name}` | `buorb_graft_image` |

This is not exhaustive. The principle: **any persistent name anywhere is in the mint universe.**

BURE tweak-name detail: the `buo` sprue is a reserved prefix for `BURE_TWEAK_NAME` values (the test-seam channel). BURE enforces the *shape* (`buo<segment>_…`) only — never specific names — so a typo'd/unregistered tweak fails loud instead of silently no-op'ing, and `grep buo` is the virtual registry (no central list). `buost_` is the reserved segment for BUK/test-stub placeholders (BUK's own, since `buobu_` is degenerate).

**What landed:**

**Verified green:** shellcheck 200 files · BUK self-test 9/9 bure-tweak cases · theurge 137/137 (incl. the sprued tweak flowing through real invocation).

For the record: the sprue + grep-registry + shape-only enforcement you steered to is genuinely better than the central enum I first reached for — it's collision-proof without coupling BUK to consumer names, and it self-documents by convention.

1. **Wrap ₢BHAAF?** It's done and verified — your call to close it (I won't auto-wrap).

Mount ₢BHAAC (lode-ensconce-collision-guard) in heat ₣BH. jjx_orient will give

ALREADY DONE & COMMITTED (a9a97c93) — only the TEST remains:
- Guard CODE: Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh, per-slot, right before
  the `skopeo copy` — inspects PKG:rbi_bole and compares its digest to the

  different => collision/fail loud. Lints clean.
- Guard SPEC: RBSLE-lode_ensconce.adoc (require + fatal). Both notched.

THE TEST'S CENTRAL SUBTLETY: ensconce mints a fresh time-based touchmark each call
(the stamp in rbld_ensconce / rbldb_Bole.sh, from BURD_NOW_STAMP, 1-second grain),
so two back-to-back CLI ensconces are minutes apart => always different Lodes =>
the guard's collision/idempotent branches never fire naturally. You must PIN the
stamp to drive two captures onto one touchmark.

PIN IT VIA THE buo TWEAK SPRUE (landed last session, ₢BHAAF — see CLAUDE.md
"BURE tweak names" + BUS0 tweak section): add a stamp-override to rbld_ensconce
honoring BURE_TWEAK_NAME=buorb_ensconce_stamp (BURE_TWEAK_VALUE = the stamp),
mirroring the made-side precedent rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (z_graft_tweak_name)
+ its rust mirror RBTDRO_GRAFT_TWEAK_NAME: define the tweak name as a bash constant,
mirror it in the rust fixture. BURE already enforces the sprue shape, so the name
must stay buo-sprued.

COLLISION CASE (add to the lode-lifecycle service fixture — rbtdrc_lode_lifecycle /
RBTDRC_CASES_LODE_LIFECYCLE in rbtdrc_crucible.rs): pin stamp S; ensconce busybox @ S
-> ensconce busybox @ S again (identical digest => passes, idempotent) -> ensconce a
DIFFERENT base @ S (debian vessel rbev-sentry-deb-tether) => collision, fails loud
-> banish to clean up. VERIFY FIRST that the debian vessel has a reliquary yoked
(skopeo comes from the reliquary; the busybox vessel does — confirm debian does too,
or yoke it).

LIVE GAR IS AUTHORIZED. Build/test theurge with tt/rbw-tb then tt/rbw-tt, then run
the fixture against live GAR (tt/rbw-tf.FixtureRun.sh lode-lifecycle) to confirm
Done. Don't auto-wrap — report and let me decide.
